analysis.fear_greed_trend: Extreme Fear for 46+ consecutive days, slowly recovering
analysis.btc_price_note: BTC testing $75K support for 3rd time in 5 days. Down ~1.59% in 24h. Failed breakout above $76K on Apr 14. Recovery from Feb 5 crash low of $60K is intact.
sentiment_key_signals: Fear & Greed Index 27 (Fear) - 46+ consecutive days in fear/extreme fear
Negative funding rates on Binance perps for 46+ days (crowded shorts, squeeze risk)
BTC failed $76K breakout on Apr 14, now retesting $75K support
ETF weekly inflows ~$1B; $663M single session on Apr 17 (IBIT + FBTC leading)
Harvard endowment, CalPERS, Goldman Sachs actively accumulating (retail vs institutional divergence)
Altcoin weakness: ETH -2.5%, SOL -3% (risk-off rotation within crypto)
Easter Sunday: reduced US/EU liquidity - thin market, potential volatile spikes
BTC dominance rising - defensive positioning into BTC from alts
March 2026 CPI: 3.3% YoY (elevated) - constrains Fed easing expectations
analysis.macro_backdrop: Fed funds 3.50-3.75% hold. CPI Mar 2026: +3.3% YoY (elevated, up from 2.4%). FOMC Apr 28-29 with 99% hold probability. No BTC ETF SEC decisions pending in next 24h. Goldman Sachs filed new BTC ETF registration. No major economic data releases April 20-21.
analysis.technical_note: Key resistance $76K-$81K. Support at $75K (100-day MA). 200-day MA at ~$87.5K indicates bearish structure vs long-term avg. Negative funding for 46 days = potential short squeeze catalyst if $76K breaks. Volume profile on any $75K breakdown is critical.
analysis.regime_rationale: Neutral: institutional buying and short-squeeze setup are bullish, but sentiment remains fearful (27), BTC failing to sustain above $76K, Easter holiday reduces liquidity, and CPI elevated constrains Fed easing. Confidence 0.42 is below 0.50 bot threshold - macro filter will NOT block trades this cycle.
